questions.q: Reduction of hexose A (molecular formula <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>12</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub></math> ) with sodium borohydride gives compound B and C. Compound B is optically inactive, whereas compound C is optically active. Which of the following is compound A?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is FALSE?
options:
Sodium ion is the major cation in the
blood plasma of vertebrates.
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Na</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> ions depress the activity of muscle enzyme.
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> ions permit the heart muscles to relax between the beats.
questions.solution:
Correct statements are:

1. Sodium ion is the major cation in the blood plasma of vertebrates.

2. Na+ ions depress the activity of muscle enzyme.

3. K+ ions permit the heart muscles to relax between the beats.

Incorrect statement is:

Na+ is not especially an enzyme activator. It helps to keep the water and electrolyte balance of the body. Sodium is also important in how nerves and muscles work
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Choose the correct Zwitter ionic form for aspartic acid.
questions.solution:
Zwitter ionic form of aspartic acid is
As, the species bear negatively charge over $\mathrm{COO}^{\ominus}$ and positivity charge over $\mathrm{H}_3 \stackrel{\oplus}{\mathrm{N}}$-; due to migration of $\mathrm{H}$-atom from $-\mathrm{COOH}$ to $-\mathrm{NH}_2$.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Dinucleotide is obtained by joining two nucleotides together by a phosphodiester linkage. Between which carbon atoms of pentose sugars of nucleotides are these linkages present?
questions.solution:
5' and 3'

Explanation: Nucleotides are joined together by phosphodiester linkage between 5' and 3' carbon atoms of the pentose sugar.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: How many tripeptides can be prepared by linking the amino acids glycine, alanine and phenyl alanine?
questions.solution:
Tripeptides are amino acids polymers in which three individual amino acid units, called residues, are linked together by amide bonds. In these, an amine group from one residue forms an amide bond with the carboxyl group of a second residue, the amino group of the second forms an amide bond with the carboxyl group of the third. Therefore, glycine $\left(\mathrm{NH}_2-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{COOH}\right)$,alanine
and phenyl alanine
can be linked in six different ways.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following statements support the cyclic form for glucose?
(i) It doesn't give Schiff's test.
(ii) It is found to exist in two different crystalline forms.
iii) It oxidises with nitric acid to give saccharic acid.
(iv) Pentaacetate of glucose doesn't react with hydroxylamine.
questions.solution: (ii) Anomeric crystalline form (i) do not give Schiff's test, because, - $\mathrm{CHO}$ group is not free. (iv) Pentaacetate do not react with hydroxylamine, because $\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{O}$ is not available. So, statements (i), (ii) and (iv) support the cyclic structure of glucose.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: A nucleoside on hydrolysis gives
options: an aldopentose and a nitrogenous base
an aldopentose, a nitrogenous base and phosphoric acid
a nitrogenous base and phosphoric acid
questions.solution:
A nucleoside on hydrolysis gives an aldopentose and a nitrogenous base.

Nucleoside is a unit formed by the attachment of a base to an aldopentose sugar.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion (A) : In aqueous solution, α-amino acids exists as internal salt called as zwitter ion.

Reason (R) : Proline is a natural amino acid having a secondary amino group.
The correct option among the following is
options: (A) is true, (R) is true and (R) is the correct explanation for (A)
(A) is true, (R) is true but (R) is not the correct explanation for (A)
questions.solution:
In aqueous solution, the carboxyl group can lose a proton and amino group can accept a proton, giving rise to a dipolar ion known as zwitter ion. This is neutral but contains both positive and negative charges.

In zwitter ionic form, amino acids show amphoteric behaviour as they react both with acids and bases.

Proline has a secondary amine group
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Primary structure in a nucleic acid contains bases as GATGC ... The chain which is complementary to this chain is
questions.solution:
Bases are paired as follows:

Cytosine (C) and Guanine (G)

Adenine (A) and Thymine (T)

So, complementary chain of GATGC..... is CTACG....

questions.q: Identify the nucleoside from the following :
questions.solution: Nucleoside consists of two basic components of nucleic acids, i.e. a pentose sugar and nitrogenous base. In this, unit base is attached at $1^{\prime}$ position of sugar and sugar carbons are numbered as $1^{\prime}, 2^{\prime}, 3^{\prime}$ etc., in order to distinguish these from the base.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following statements are correct?
(A) A tripeptide had two peptide bonds.
(B) A pentapeptide contains five amino acids.
(C) Nucleotide is a product of base and sugar.
(D) In cellulose, \(\boldsymbol{\beta}\)-glycosidic linkages are present.
questions.solution:
The explanations of given statements are as follows:
(A) A tripetide contains 3 amino acids linked by two peptide linkages. Thus, statement (A) is correct.
(B) A pentapeptide contains five amino acids linked by four peptide linkages. Thus, statement (B) is also correct.
(C) Nucleoside is a product of base and sugar. Thus, statements (C) is incorrect.
(D) In cellulose, \(\beta\)-D-glucose units are joined by glycosidic linkage between \(\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{l}\) of, one glucose and C-4 of the next glucose unit. Thus, statement (D) is a also correct.
Hence, option (c) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Hardening of oil is done by
questions.solution: $\underset{\text { (unsaturated) }}{\text { Oil }} \stackrel{\text { Hydrogenation }}{\longrightarrow} \begin{array}{c}\text { ghee } \\ \text { (saturated) }\end{array}$ This is called hardening of oil.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A polypeptide can be called as protein when its mass is
questions.solution: Peptides containing more than 10 u amino acids are called polypeptides. Polypeptides are formed by the linear sequence of amino acids. Some proteins are composed of two or more poly peptide chains. Relatively shorter peptides are known as oligo peptides whereas longer polymers are called polypeptides. Polypeptides containing more than 100 u amino acids having molecular mass higher than 10,000 u are generally called as proteins. However, the distinctive between a polypeptide and a protein is not sharp.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: mRNA forms about___of the total cellular RNA.
questions.solution: Messanger RNA covers for only 3–5% of the complete cellular RNA. The actual amount of mRNA depends on the type of cell and physiological state of cell. Approximately 3 lakh 60 thousand mRNA molecules are present in a single cell of mammal, which is approximately made up of twelve thousand different transcripts with a typical length of around 2 kb.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.solution: Amylose is a polysaccharide which is made of $\alpha$-D glucose units bonded through $\alpha(1-\mathrm{H})$ glycoside linkage. So, the correct option is 1 .

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is not true about DNA and RNA?
options: Unlike DNA, RNA is almost always a single-stranded molecule and has a much shorter chain of nucleotides.
RNA contains ribose, rather than the deoxyribose found in DNA
DNA is a long polymer of nucleotides (a polynucleotide) that encodes the sequence of amino acid residues in proteins, using the genetic code.
DNA is less stable than RNA
questions.solution:
Unlike DNA, RNA is almost always a single-stranded molecule and has a much shorter chain of nucleotides. RNA contains ribose, rather than the deoxyribose found in DNA (there is a hydroxyl group attached to the pentose ring in the 2 ' position whereas RNA has two hydroxyl groups). These hydroxyl groups make RNA less stable than DNA because it is more prone to hydrolysis. Several types of RNA (tRNA, rRNA) contain a great deal of secondary structure, which help promote stability.

Deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) is a nucleic acid that contains the genetic instructions for the biological development of a cellular form of life or a virus. All known cellular life and some viruses have DNAs. DNA is a long polymer of nucleotides (a polynucleotide) that encodes the sequence of amino acid residues in proteins, using the genetic code.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion (A): The denaturation of proteins can destroy all 1°,2° and 3° protein structures.

Reason (R): Curdling of milk is due to denaturation of proteins.

The correct option among the following is
options: (A) is true, (R) is true and (R) is the correct explanation for (A)
(A) is true, (R) is true but (R) is not the correct explanation for (A)
questions.solution:
Protein found in a biological system with a unique three-dimensional structure and biological activity is called a native protein. When a protein in its native form, is subjected to physical change like change in temperature or chemical change like change in pH, the hydrogen bonds are disturbed. Due to this, globules unfold and helix get uncoiled and protein loses its biological activity. This is called denaturation of protein.

The coagulation of egg white on boiling is a common example of denaturation. Another example is curdling of milk which is caused due to the formation of lactic acid by the bacteria present in milk.

During denaturation secondary and tertiary structures are destroyed but primary structure remains intact.

Hence,(A) is false but (R) is true
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Which of the following amino acids is basic in nature?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: Starch on complete hydrolysis gives fructose
Lactose on hydrolysis gives glucose and fructose
Glucose on slow oxidation to $\mathrm{CO}_2$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ by enzyme does not liberate energy
Cellulose is not digestible in human body
questions.solution: Cellulose is a polysaccharide which holds or binds many small monosaccharide units together by glycosidic bonds. The type of glycosidic bond present is of $(\beta)-1,4$ bond. Which cannot be broken down by enzymes present in human body. Thus, we cannot digest cellulose.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following disaccharide will not reduce Tollen's reagent?
questions.solution: In (Q), it could be found that both the pyranose rings are attached through their reducing group, hence it will not be hydrolysed, and it can not reduce Tollen's reagent. So (Q) is not a reducing sugar, whereas in (P) one ring is hemiacetal which can be hydrolysed therefore it will be hydrolysed in solution and can reduce Tollen's reagent.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following statements are true about carbohydrates?
(i) Monosaccharides can be hydrolysed.
(ii) The two monosaccharide units obtained on hydrolysis of a disaccharide can either be same or different.
(iii) Polysaccharides are now sweet in taste.
(iv) All monosaccharides are not reducing. sugars.
questions.solution:
(i) Monosaccharides are smallest carbohydrates that cannot be hydrolysed.
So, statement (i) is not true.
(ii) Disaccharides are made up of two units of monosaccharides, so their hydrolysis will also give two monosaccharide unit that can be same or different.
e.g., Sucrose $\longrightarrow$ Glucose + Fructose
Maltose $\longrightarrow$ Glucose + Glucose
So, statement (ii) is true.
(iii) Polysaccharides are polymers of many monosaccharides and have no sweet taste.
So, statements (iii) is true.
(iv) Monosaccharides are categorised as reducing and non-reducing sugars.
So, statement (iv) is not true.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Which one is not a protein?
questions.solution:
The haematin is a bluish-black acid derived from hemoglobin by removal of the protein part and oxidation of the iron atom. It contains iron in the ferric state.

Actin, collagen and albumin are proteins.

questions.q: Identify the product of the following reaction.
questions.solution:
The starch is a polysaccharide. It gives the monosaccharide on the hydrolysis. So, it formed the glucose on the hydrolysis.

Reaction:

C6H10O5n + nH2O →393 K, 2-3 atmH+ C6H12O6(Glucose)

Hence, the product of the above reaction is glucose.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion (A) Adenine and guanine are present in RNA as purine bases.

Reason (R) The base uracil is present in RNA.

The correct option among the following is
options: $(A)$ is true, $(R)$ is true and $(R)$ is the correct explanation for $(A)$.
$(A)$ is true, $(R)$ is true but $(R)$ is not the correct explanation for $(A)$.
questions.solution:
RNA contains four bases :
The purines - adenine (A) and guanine $(G)$.
The pyrimidines - cytosine (C) and uracil (U).
The bases can interact via hydrogen bonds.
So, (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following statements about protein structure is correct ?
options: Proteins consisting of one polypeptide can have quaternary structure
The formation of a disulphide bond in a protein requires that the two participating cystine residues be adjacent to each other in the primary sequence of the protein.
The stability of quaternary structure in proteins is mainly due to covalent bonds among the subunits.
The information required for the correct folding of a protein is contained in the specific sequence of amino acids along the polypeptide chain.
questions.solution: The correct folding of a protein is guided by specific interactions among the side chains of the amino acid residues of a polypeptide chain. The two cystine residues that react to form the disulphide bond may be a great distance apart in the primary structure (or on separate polypeptides) but are brought into close proximity by the three - dimension folding of the polypeptide chain.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Identify the correct statement related to amino acids
options: Nonessential amino acids cannot be synthesized in the body
These are low melting solid substances
In aqueous solution they exist as zwitter ion
questions.solution: Essential amino acids cannot be synthesized in our body so we need to take them in our diet. Amino acids are soluble in water but generally insoluble in organic solvents. Due to crystalline nature, they are high melting solids because of dipole-dipole interactions. They exist as dipolar zwitter ions in aqueous solutions.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
